label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about which statement is true regarding folk medicine based on the given passage. The passage starts by explaining that folk medicine uses plants and herbs to keep the body healthy. It mentions that materials for making medicines can be found everywhere, so it can be practiced anywhere in the world. Then it goes back to early human history, saying that both humans and animals used plants and herbs. It also talks about how humans learned from observing animals, like how they rest and use plants when sick. There's a part that says some people think folk medicine includes old wives' tales and fairy tales, which should be separated from it. Now, looking at the options: A) It keeps one healthy by using medicines taken from plants and herbs. This seems to directly match the first sentence of the passage. The passage says folk medicine keeps the body healthy using medicines from surrounding plants and herbs. So A looks correct. B) It can treat most kinds of diseases for both man and animals. The passage does mention that animals use plants, but the passage doesn't explicitly say it can treat "most" diseases for both. The passage also says some stories are fairy tales, so maybe not all treatments are effective. So B might not be correct. C) It is better than other medicines in curing serious diseases. The passage doesn't compare folk medicine to other types of medicine. There's no mention of it being better. So C is likely incorrect. D) It was a way of
